gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An immutable persistent indexed sequence (value-semantic; spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Iteration order is **index order** (0, 1, …, len-1) — a documented, stable property"] # [doc = " of the type (the no-silent-reorder crux from RFC-0016 §4.4)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq , Hash)] pub struct Seq < E > { # [doc = " Internal storage. `Arc`-based sharing is deferred (spec is design-phase);"] # [doc = " a plain `Vec` gives the correct value semantics and passes the guarantee matrix."] # [doc = " Structural sharing is an *implementation* property invisible to identity (C4)."] inner : Vec < E > , }
gaps.reason: struct `Seq` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl < E : Clone > Seq < E > { # [doc = " An empty `Seq`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn empty () -> Self { Seq { inner : Vec :: new () } } # [doc = " Construct a `Seq` from a slice of elements (cloned)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn from_slice (elems : & [E]) -> Self { Seq { inner : elems . to_vec () , } } # [doc = " The number of elements."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn len (& self) -> usize { self . inner . len () } # [doc = " `true` iff the sequence is empty."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn is_empty (& self) -> bool { self . inner . is_empty () } # [doc = " The element at index `i`, or `None` when `i >= len` (C1 — never a default)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`. Fallibility: `None` when `i >= len`."] # [must_use] pub fn get (& self , i : usize) -> Option < & E > { self . inner . get (i) } # [doc = " The first element, or `None` on empty (C1 — never a default)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`. Fallibility: `None` on empty."] # [must_use] pub fn first (& self) -> Option < & E > { self . inner . first () } # [doc = " Append `x` to the back, returning a new `Seq`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The input `Seq` is not modified (value semantics, C4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn push (& self , x : E) -> Self { let mut new_inner = self . inner . clone () ; new_inner . push (x) ; Seq { inner : new_inner } } # [doc = " Remove the last element, returning `Some((new_seq, element))`,"] # [doc = " or `None` on empty (C1 — never a silent no-op)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`. Fallibility: `None` on empty."] # [must_use] pub fn pop (& self) -> Option < (Self , E) > { if self . inner . is_empty () { None } else { let mut new_inner = self . inner . clone () ; let elem = new_inner . pop () . expect ("inner is non-empty") ; Some ((Seq { inner : new_inner } , elem)) } } # [doc = " Replace element at index `i` with `x`, returning a new `Seq`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `Err(IndexOOB)` when `i >= len` (C1 — never a silent clamp or default)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN (C3)"] # [doc = " The `Err(IndexOOB { index, len, context })` is the reified refusal record"] # [doc = " (RFC-0013 structured diagnostic): `index`, `len`, and `context` are inspectable."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`. Fallibility: `Err(IndexOOB)` when `i >= len`."] pub fn update (& self , i : usize , x : E) -> Result < Self , CollErr > { let len = self . inner . len () ; if i >= len { return Err (CollErr :: index_oob (i , len , "i >= len")) ; } let mut new_inner = self . inner . clone () ; new_inner [i] = x ; Ok (Seq { inner : new_inner }) } # [doc = " Concatenate `self` (then `other`), returning a new `Seq`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Order: `self` elements first, then `other` elements (documented)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn concat (& self , other : & Self) -> Self { let mut new_inner = self . inner . clone () ; new_inner . extend_from_slice (& other . inner) ; Seq { inner : new_inner } } # [doc = " Return the sub-sequence `[lo, hi)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `Err(IndexOOB)` when `lo > hi` or `hi > len` — never silently clamps"] # [doc = " (C1 — the \"no silent clamp\" guarantee)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN (C3)"] # [doc = " The refusal record names the violated constraint in `context`:"] # [doc = " `\"lo > hi\"` or `\"hi > len\"`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`. Fallibility: `Err(IndexOOB)` on `lo > hi` or `hi > len`."] pub fn slice (& self , lo : usize , hi : usize) -> Result < Self , CollErr > { let len = self . inner . len () ; if lo > hi { return Err (CollErr :: index_oob (lo , hi , "lo > hi")) ; } if hi > len { return Err (CollErr :: index_oob (hi , len , "hi > len")) ; } Ok (Seq { inner : self . inner [lo .. hi] . to_vec () , }) } # [doc = " Iterate elements in **index order** (0, 1, …, len-1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the documented-order promise (the no-silent-reorder crux). The order is"] # [doc = " a property of the type, not of the build or insertion history."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact`, total."] # [must_use] pub fn foldable (& self) -> & [E] { & self . inner } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use crate :: error :: CollErr ; fn seq123 () -> Seq < i32 > { Seq :: from_slice (& [1 , 2 , 3]) } # [doc = " `len` / `is_empty` are `Exact` and total."] # [test] fn len_and_is_empty_are_total () { let s : Seq < i32 > = Seq :: empty () ; assert_eq ! (s . len () , 0) ; assert ! (s . is_empty ()) ; let s2 = s . push (42) ; assert_eq ! (s2 . len () , 1) ; assert ! (! s2 . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " `get` returns `None` for out-of-range, never panics (C1)."] # [test] fn get_returns_none_when_out_of_range () { let s = seq123 () ; assert_eq ! (s . get (0) , Some (& 1)) ; assert_eq ! (s . get (2) , Some (& 3)) ; assert_eq ! (s . get (3) , None) ; assert_eq ! (s . get (usize :: MAX) , None) ; } # [doc = " `first` returns `None` on empty (C1)."] # [test] fn first_returns_none_on_empty () { let s : Seq < i32 > = Seq :: empty () ; assert_eq ! (s . first () , None) ; let s2 = s . push (7) ; assert_eq ! (s2 . first () , Some (& 7)) ; } # [doc = " `push` returns a new `Seq`; the original is unchanged (C4)."] # [test] fn push_returns_new_seq_original_unchanged () { let s = seq123 () ; let s2 = s . push (4) ; assert_eq ! (s . len () , 3 , "original must be unchanged after push (C4)") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . len () , 4) ; assert_eq ! (s2 . get (3) , Some (& 4)) ; } # [doc = " `pop` returns a new `Seq`; the original is unchanged (C4)."] # [test] fn pop_returns_new_seq_original_unchanged () { let s = seq123 () ; let (s2 , elem) = s . pop () . expect ("non-empty") ; assert_eq ! (s . len () , 3 , "original must be unchanged after pop (C4)") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . len () , 2) ; assert_eq ! (elem , 3) ; } # [doc = " `pop` on empty returns `None` — never a silent no-op (C1)."] # [test] fn pop_returns_none_on_empty () { let s : Seq < i32 > = Seq :: empty () ; assert_eq ! (s . pop () , None) ; } # [doc = " `update` returns a new `Seq`; the original is unchanged (C4)."] # [test] fn update_returns_new_seq_original_unchanged () { let s = seq123 () ; let s2 = s . update (1 , 99) . expect ("valid index") ; assert_eq ! (s . get (1) , Some (& 2) , "original must be unchanged (C4)") ; assert_eq ! (s2 . get (1) , Some (& 99)) ; } # [doc = " `update` returns `Err(IndexOOB)` when `i >= len` (C1)."] # [test] fn update_returns_err_when_oob () { let s = seq123 () ; let err = s . update (3 , 99) . unwrap_err () ; assert ! (matches ! (err , CollErr :: IndexOOB { index : 3 , len : 3 , .. }) , "must be IndexOOB with index=3 len=3, got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " Property: `update(i, x)` succeeds iff `i < len`; fails with `IndexOOB` iff `i >= len`."] # [doc = " Guard: a clamping update (returning the unmodified seq instead of Err) makes this fail."] # [test] fn update_succeeds_iff_i_lt_len () { let s = seq123 () ; let len = s . len () ; for i in 0 .. len { assert ! (s . update (i , 0) . is_ok () , "update({i}) must succeed when i < len={len}") ; } for i in [len , len + 1 , usize :: MAX] { let r = s . update (i , 0) ; assert ! (r . is_err () , "update({i}) must fail when i >= len={len} (C1 never-silent)") ; } } # [doc = " `concat` preserves order: `a ‖ b` — not `b ‖ a` (documented order)."] # [test] fn concat_order_is_a_then_b () { let a = Seq :: from_slice (& [1 , 2]) ; let b = Seq :: from_slice (& [3 , 4]) ; let ab = a . concat (& b) ; assert_eq ! (ab . foldable () , & [1 , 2 , 3 , 4]) ; let ba = b . concat (& a) ; assert_eq ! (ba . foldable () , & [3 , 4 , 1 , 2]) ; assert_ne ! (ab , ba , "order must matter (documented concat order)") ; } # [doc = " `slice(lo, hi)` with `lo == hi` gives an empty sub-sequence."] # [test] fn slice_empty_range_returns_empty () { let s = seq123 () ; let empty = s . slice (1 , 1) . expect ("valid empty range") ; assert ! (empty . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " `slice` returns `Err(IndexOOB)` when `lo > hi` (C1 — no silent clamp)."] # [test] fn slice_err_when_lo_gt_hi () { let s = seq123 () ; let err = s . slice (2 , 1) . unwrap_err () ; assert ! (matches ! (err , CollErr :: IndexOOB { context : "lo > hi" , .. }) , "must be IndexOOB with context='lo > hi', got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " `slice` returns `Err(IndexOOB)` when `hi > len` (C1 — no silent clamp)."] # [test] fn slice_err_when_hi_gt_len () { let s = seq123 () ; let err = s . slice (0 , 4) . unwrap_err () ; assert ! (matches ! (err , CollErr :: IndexOOB { context : "hi > len" , .. }) , "must be IndexOOB with context='hi > len', got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " Property: `slice(lo, hi)` succeeds iff `lo <= hi <= len`."] # [doc = " Guard: a silently-clamped slice makes this fail."] # [test] fn slice_succeeds_iff_valid_bounds () { let s = seq123 () ; let len = s . len () ; for lo in 0 ..= len { for hi in lo ..= len { assert ! (s . slice (lo , hi) . is_ok () , "slice({lo},{hi}) must succeed with lo<=hi<=len={len}") ; } } assert ! (s . slice (2 , 1) . is_err () , "slice(2,1) must fail: lo > hi (C1)") ; assert ! (s . slice (0 , len + 1) . is_err () , "slice(0, len+1) must fail: hi > len (C1)") ; } # [doc = " `foldable` iterates in index order (documented order, no silent reorder)."] # [test] fn foldable_walks_in_index_order () { let s = seq123 () ; let elems : Vec < i32 > = s . foldable () . to_vec () ; assert_eq ! (elems , [1 , 2 , 3] , "foldable must walk in index order (spec §3)") ; } # [doc = " `foldable` of an empty seq is empty."] # [test] fn foldable_of_empty_is_empty () { let s : Seq < i32 > = Seq :: empty () ; assert ! (s . foldable () . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " Property: two `Seq`s built with the same elements in the same order are equal"] # [doc = " and their `foldable` slices are equal — insertion history with the same final"] # [doc = " contents yields the same observable sequence."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guard: any non-determinism in `push` ordering makes this fail."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Note: this property is tagged `Declared` in the spec (§4/§7-Q2 — to be promoted"] # [doc = " to `Empirical` once code lands). It is checked here as a deterministic test"] # [doc = " (fixed elements, no randomness), which satisfies the property-test obligation"] # [doc = " (VR-5: do not claim `Proven` without a theorem)."] # [test] fn equal_contents_yield_equal_foldable_order () { let s1 = Seq :: empty () . push (1) . push (2) . push (3) ; let s2 = Seq :: from_slice (& [1 , 2 , 3]) ; let s3 = Seq :: from_slice (& [1]) . concat (& Seq :: from_slice (& [2 , 3])) ; assert_eq ! (s1 . foldable () , s2 . foldable () , "different construction paths with same contents must yield same foldable order") ; assert_eq ! (s1 . foldable () , s3 . foldable () , "concat path must yield same foldable order (no silent reorder)") ; } # [doc = " Property: push then pop round-trips the element."] # [doc = " Guard: a push or pop that discards the element makes this fail."] # [test] fn push_pop_round_trip () { let s = seq123 () ; let s2 = s . push (99) ; let (s3 , elem) = s2 . pop () . expect ("non-empty after push") ; assert_eq ! (elem , 99 , "pop must return the last pushed element") ; assert_eq ! (s3 . len () , s . len () , "round-trip must restore original length") ; assert_eq ! (s3 . foldable () , s . foldable () , "round-trip must restore original contents") ; } }
